institution.name: SUNY Downstate / University Hospital of Brooklyn

searchTermsTried: SUNY Downstate College of Medicine visiting medical student VSLO international University Hospital of Brooklyn

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Medical Student Program | Registrar | SUNY Downstate
candidateFindings.shortQuote: SUNY Downstate only accepts students in their senior year from LCME-accredited allopathic schools in the United States, Canada and Puerto Rico with the exception of some departments which accept students from College of Osteopathic.
candidateFindings.durationDecision: Senior year only; 45-day lead time pre-rotation
candidateFindings.specialtyDecision: Multispecialty; primarily LCME with some departments accepting COCA

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Procedures for Visiting International Medical Students | SUNY Downstate
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Rotations at SUNY Downstate Health Sciences University - College of Medicine are currently closed for international medical students.

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: International medical student rotations at SUNY Downstate are 'currently closed' verbatim — important caveat. Even the Brooklyn safety-net AMC label doesn't make it IMG-open at present.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: University Hospital of Brooklyn not separately named (it's the SUNY Downstate teaching hospital so implicitly covered). Cost and visa absent.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) Senior-year LCME US/Canada/PR (+some depts COCA); (b) international lane CURRENTLY CLOSED verbatim; (c) VSLO/VSAS only with 45-day lead time; (d) cost/visa NOT_STATED.
plainEnglishSummary: SUNY Downstate / University Hospital of Brooklyn accepts visiting medical students through SUNY Downstate's Registrar via VSLO. Senior-year students from LCME-accredited schools in US/Canada/Puerto Rico (some departments also COCA-DO). International medical school rotations are CURRENTLY CLOSED (verbatim). 45-day lead time, no exceptions. Cost and visa not stated on the hub.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because NY gained a fourth P101 packet; SUNY Downstate's currently-closed international lane captured verbatim — means no IMG-friendly label may be applied to SUNY Downstate based on its safety-net status.
